phrases.intent_en: Ask for the best spot to photograph the bridge
phrases.text_local: Quel est le meilleur endroit pour photographier le pont ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel eh luh may-YUR ahn-DRWAH poor foh-toh-grah-FYAY luh POHN
phrases.cultural_note: The Pont de Pierre, commissioned by Napoleon, was the first permanent bridge over the Garonne in Bordeaux.

responses.english: From the quay, across the way.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if it is safe to walk across the bridge
phrases.text_local: Est-il sécurisé de traverser le pont à pied ?
phrases.pronunciation: eh-TEEL say-kyoo-ree-ZAY duh trah-vair-SAY luh POHN ah PYAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Pont de Pierre has a dedicated pedestrian walkway alongside car lanes; cyclists are also permitted.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the bridge is lit up at night
phrases.text_local: Le pont est-il illuminé le soir ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh POHN eh-TEEL ee-loo-mee-NAY luh SWAHR
phrases.cultural_note: The Pont de Pierre is beautifully illuminated at night, making for excellent evening photography from the quays.

responses.english: Yes, it's very beautiful at night.
responses.phonetic: wee, seh treh BOH luh SWAHR
responses.french: Oui, c'est très beau le soir.

phrases.intent_en: Ask a local if the view is better from this side or the other bank
phrases.text_local: Selon vous, la vue est-elle plus belle de ce côté ou de l'autre rive ?
phrases.pronunciation: suh-LOHN voo lah VYU eh-TEL plyoo BEL duh suh koh-TAY oo duh LOH-truh REEV
phrases.cultural_note: The right bank (Rive Droite) offers a panoramic view of the Bordeaux classical waterfront skyline.

responses.english: The other side, the view is prettier.
responses.phonetic: LOH-truh koh-TAY, lah VOO eh ploo BEL
responses.french: L'autre côté, la vue est plus belle.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what time sunset is today
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure est le coucher du soleil aujourd'hui ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel ur eh luh koo-SHAY doo soh-LAY oh-zhoor-DWEE
phrases.cultural_note: Sunsets over the Garonne from the Pont de Pierre are particularly dramatic when the sky is reflected in the wide tidal river.
